fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Delaware Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Delaware - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity delaware, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to delaware, pharmacy technician compact delaware, delaware pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Delaware Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address:
Division of Professional Regulation
Cannon Building, Suite 203
861 Silver Lake Blvd.
Dover, DE 19904

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 2 hours of continuing education every two years

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Delaware.

steps.title: 1. Create a DELPROS Account
steps.description: Navigate to Delaware's Professional Licensure System (DELPROS) at https://delpro.delaware.gov/ and register for a new account if you do not already have one.

steps.title: 2. Start the Application
steps.description: Log into your DELPROS account. From your dashboard, click 'Apply for a License' and select the 'Pharmacy Technician' application type from the available options.

steps.title: 3. Complete Application & Upload Documents
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application within DELPROS. Upload necessary documents such as your high school diploma/GED, proof of age (18+), proof of passing a Board-approved certification exam (e.g., PTCB), or proof of enrollment in a Board-approved training program. You will also need to list all jurisdictions where you have held a professional license.

steps.title: 4. Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $20 and the Prescription Monitoring Program (PMP) registration fee of $25 through the DELPROS portal using an accepted payment method.

steps.title: 5. Complete Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: After submitting your application, you will receive instructions and a consent form for a state and federal criminal background check. Follow these instructions promptly to complete the CBC process, as it is required for licensure.

steps.title: 6. Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your application status within your DELPROS account for any updates or requests for additional information from the Delaware Board of Pharmacy. The Board will notify you once your license is approved.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Delaware offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the Delaware Board of Pharmacy for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Delaware by submitting a new application to the Delaware Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Delaware-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a Delaware pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Delaware have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Delaware. To work in Delaware, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Delaware?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Delaware while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.endorsement_url: https://delpro.delaware.gov/ (Select 'Apply for a License' and choose 'Pharmacy Technician via Reciprocity' application type)
reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Delaware by submitting a new application to the Delaware Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Delaware-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.
